payload.text:
### Guidance for Search Engine Indexing:

1.  **Meta Tags**: I have added the standard `description` and `keywords` tags to your `index.html`. These are the primary sources for search engines to understand what your site is about.
2.  **Favicon**: I have explicitly defined `shortcut icon` and `apple-touch-icon` links pointing to your logo URL. Search engines use these to display your brand icon next to search results.
3.  **Open Graph (Social Sharing)**: I added `og:title`, `og:description`, and `og:image`. This ensures that when your new referral links (`webappURL/signup?ref=...`) are shared on platforms like WhatsApp, Twitter, or Facebook, they will display a nice preview card with your logo and description.
4.  **Google Search Console**: To get indexed faster, you should verify your domain (`app.trustrium.com`) with **Google Search Console** and submit your sitemap (if you have one, or just inspect your main URL to request indexing). Since your app is a Single Page Application (SPA), Google can crawl it, but ensure your `AuthPage` (the landing page for guests) has relevant text content visible.

payload.text:
### Guide for Search Engine Indexing:

1.  **Google Search Console**: To get your site indexed faster, verify your domain (`app.trustrium.com`) with **Google Search Console**. You can do this by adding a DNS TXT record or uploading an HTML file (if your hosting provider supports it).
2.  **Sitemap**: Since this is a single-page app (SPA), your "pages" (like `/signup`, `/about`) are virtual. Google is smart enough to crawl Javascript, but ensuring you have a `sitemap.xml` listing your main routes can help.
3.  **Favicon Indexing**: I have standardized the favicon links in your `index.html`. Google looks for these stable links to display your icon next to search results. It may take a few days or weeks for Google to update the favicon in search results after re-crawling.
4.  **Metadata**: The `description` and `keywords` tags I added are crucial. Ensure the description accurately reflects your app's value proposition as this is what users will see in search results.

### **SEO & Indexing Guide**
To get your app indexed on Google:
1.  **Google Search Console (GSC)**: Go to [search.google.com/search-console](https://search.google.com/search-console), add your domain (`app.trustrium.com`), and verify ownership (usually via DNS record).
2.  **Submit Sitemap**: Once verified in GSC, go to the "Sitemaps" section and submit `https://app.trustrium.com/sitemap.xml`.
3.  **Request Indexing**: In GSC, inspect your main URL (`https://app.trustrium.com`) and click "Request Indexing".
4.  **Social Signals**: Share your new links on the social platforms you provided; traffic and backlinks help indexing speed.

To fix the "Sitemap is HTML" error in Google Search Console, we need to modify the routing configuration.

**The Problem:**
Your current `vercel.json` configuration rewrites **all** requests (`/(.*)`) to your `index.html` (the React app). This is standard for Single Page Applications, but it incorrectly intercepts requests for static files like `sitemap.xml` and `robots.txt`, serving the HTML app instead of the actual XML/Text file.
